title: olearia imbricata
intro: Olearia imbricata, also known as the New Zealand flax-leaved daisy bush or flax-leaved daisy, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, Olearia imbricata can be made into a tea, tincture, or salve, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb.
